## Patch notes/Oct 03 2025
## Summary 
This pre-patch update prepares the game for Theatre of Blood release while introducing a completely redesigned achievements interface with filters and search functionality. The update also fixes critical issues with 2-handed weapon damage, adds visual indicators for sunder armour stacks, and includes numerous quality-of-life improvements and bug fixes.

## ToB Release Pre-patch 
Ahead of time, this patch brings ToB to the main game, alongside a bunch of other fixes and changes.

## Changes and fixes 
* The weird messages you get when equipping items has been removed
* Your damage with 2h weapons should be back to normal! With the previous patch, there was an issue with detecting if your weapon was 2 handed
* HP Bars for raid bosses/demibosses/minions have been updated to be a bit bigger
* Sundering armour now has a visual indication to show you how many stacks are left til the target has reached max sunder stacks. Also, each sunder stack added will show an armour hitsplat with how many stacks are added
* Sunder armour also apparently hasn't been working for the past year, so now it is
* TDs smouldering common items are now tradeable
* All of the items that previously were tradeable that became untradeable are now... tradeable!
* Blazing blowpipe spec now heals
* Burning claws will now be added to the collection log when you put them together

## Achievements Interface Redesign 
The achievements interface has been completely changed! Should be A LOT more usable, with some quick filters on the side and a search bar!

## Additional fixes 
* Slayer locked accounts can get a TOB slayer task at P4 slayer
* TDs won't show double KC in the collection log anymore
* The TOB leaderboard won't take the place of the League Point leaderboard anymore, it'll be a bit off to the side so you can view both on leagues worlds
* ToB top 3 leaderboard entries will show the exact number of ticks that it took to complete the raid, just in case it needs to come down to that

## Patch notes/Oct 04 2024
## Update from Oct 04 2024 
This major update introduces the Tombs of Amascut raid with four boss encounters (Akkha, Baba, Kephri, and Zebak) and numerous powerful rewards including Masori Armor, Osmumten's Fang, and Tumeken's Shadow. The update also includes various fixes for Halloween items, equipment animations, and other quality-of-life improvements.

## The Tombs of Amascut release 
It's finally time to delve into the tombs, and here's what to expect.

## Raid mechanics 
* Choose to battle Akkha, Baba, Kephri, or Zebak
* We wanted to give you this choice so that you can choose the boss you'd like, then you can trade and barter with others for the bosses you're not as keen for
* Each boss will have its own key fragment that you can trade amongst yourselves
* Collect 1 fragment from each boss, then you can combine it into a key to open up the purple chest west of ::home!

## Bonus loot 
* Every boss has the chance to drop a bonus common loot key
* This is used to get 3 rolls on the normal boss drop table, plus rolls for the Keris partisan
* If you're lucky, you can get purple key fragments, or even a full purple key!

## The rewards 

## Masori Crafting Kit 
* An uncommon drop that's used in the crafting recipes for fortifying Masori Armour and the Masori Assembler

## Scarab Dung 
* Used in a new herblore product
* Lilys of the sand + herbal mixture (unf) can be combined at the alchemy table to make a herbal mixture
* Then the Scarab dung is used with that potion to make a Fertilizing mixture
* When you harvest crops with the fertilizing mixture in your inventory, you gain 1 extra product per farming harvest

## Eldritch Ashes 
* Can be scattered for some prayer experience

## Big banana 
* A stackable food that can heal a small amount

## Fang 
* Can be fletched into bones for a small amount of experience

## Masori Armor 
* An all around good ranged armor with decent defences

## Masori Armor (f) 
* An upgraded masori in terms of offences
* Has no defence

## Patch notes/Oct 07 2025
## Summary 
This update addresses several bugs affecting Theatre of Blood and other boss mechanics, including Xarpus reflection issues, Baba boulder locks, TOB damage percentage tracking, and fixes an issue where changing equipment would interrupt chest looting.

## Bug fixes 
* Xarpus should no longer appear to face the wrong way and still reflect damage at you
* Zammy GWD minions should no longer be able to be spawned in slayer instances
* Baba boulders should no longer hard-lock you in certain situations where you get smacked by them
* TOB bosses should now tell you the damage percentage you dealt towards them, even though they don't drop loot
* Slayer locked players should be able to attack Verzik nylos now
* Taking off your pants (or changing equipment at all) should no longer interrupt things like looting a chest

## Patch notes/Oct 08 2024
## Update from Oct 08 2024 
This update addresses several bugs and makes improvements to tempering mechanics, combat interactions, and collection log entries. The update also adds enhanced logging to help troubleshoot disconnection issues and hints at upcoming Halloween content.

## Bug fixes and improvements 
* The World Boss Arena events plugin should now properly update when there isn't a world boss currently spawned
* Tempering items now only works on tiered items, gives 3% extra stats per rank instead of 1.5%, and can be tempered up to 4 times
* Apollo's explosion attack (when too many energy spawns heal Apollo) should no longer hit you while you're outside of the instance
* Player options like "Attack" and "Challenge" should now be cleared properly when leaving the area they were added in
* Poseidon's Tassets are now in the Olympian Raid collection log
* More logging has been put in place across a bunch of places to try and get more information about disconnections
* A bunch more halloween stuff you should be seeing soon

## Patch notes/Oct 09 2024
## Update from Oct 09 2024 
This major update introduces Breaches, an entirely new game mechanic featuring chaotic multi-combat areas with optional PvP and special jackpot drops. The update also launches the Halloween event with a Trick or Treat system, a new world boss named Jhallan, a spooky home map, and various seasonal rewards. Additionally, the voting system has been revamped to provide better incentives.

## Breaches 
Breaches are a whole new mechanic coming to the game, where we take existing and new bosses, and throw them all in a limited area, for an absolutely HECTIC experience.

* You can choose to opt-in to PvP for increased drop rate, choosing the level of risk and reward you'd like to take on
* HCIMs and PDHCIMs are safe to die in the breach without worrying about losing status
* Kill Streaks will reward you with even more increased drop rate while you can maintain it
* There's a special jackpot drop table which you can hit for meeting participation thresholds (minimum 10% of the damage done to an NPC), which are full of REALLY JUICY loot! While there's a breach queued up, use ::jackpot to see the table for that breach!
* Transmogs will be hidden inside PvP breaches

Breaches are entirely multicombat, and a chaotic free-for-all. You may want to find some friends to group up with if you're planning on PvPing!

## Halloween Festivities 

## Trick or Treat 
South of home, have a chat with Death and do some Trick or Treating!

Once a day you can get a treat from Death, giving you one of many status effects for a longer period of time! Use it or lose it!

Uncapped by time, choose to take Easy, Medium, or Hard tasks from Death to gain Spooky Sweets: the currency used for the Halloween store!

Death's tasks will range from delivering (and giving away) items to specific NPCs around the map, killing some amount of a specific NPC, gathering a certain amount of a specific resource, or crafting a certain amount of a specific item!

Get rewarded according to the d
[... 1723 more chars ...]

## Patch notes/Oct 11 2024
## Update from Oct 11 2024 
This update focuses on improving the Halloween event experience with numerous quality-of-life features for Trick tasks and Breach gameplay. Key changes include group participation in Trick tasks, improved item tradability, more ways to earn spooky sweets, and enhanced Breach mechanics with better NPC spawning and reduced damage contribution requirements.

## Halloween event changes 

## Trick tasks 
* Trick tasks which require killing NPCs can now be done in groups, with the only requirement being that you need to have done at least 20% of the damage to the NPC
* The Halloween Mystery Box is now tradeable
* The Trinket of Vengeance now has a description of how it works in its examine text
* Vorkath kills now count towards Trick tasks
* Tasks which cannot be completed, like the olympian logs one, can now be disabled live, and they will be able to be reset for free if you have a disabled task
* The daily treat cooldown will only be set when you get the buff if you somehow cancel the dialog
* You can now progress Halloween Trick tasks when you gain items via Infernal Gatherer and Grace of the Elves
* The Phantasm and Necrotic cape, boots, macuahuitls, cane and wand - and pumpkin/inverted-pumpkin santa hats have been added into the Vote shop and Donator Cosmetic shop
* Jhallan's start to the boss fight should perform a lot better now, particularly in Breaches, where he'd lag the world with the explosion he does
* Bob the cat slippers have been added to the Halloween shop, and spiked manacles can now be equipped
* A "Death Note" has been added to the Halloween shop for 1 spooky sweet, which allows you to check your current task progress wherever you are
* Trick tasks can now be cancelled with coins instead of sweets. Hard tasks are intentionally much more expensive to cancel
* Slayer tasks will now give you a small amount of spooky sweets upon completion
* A bunch of cosmetic items for the Halloween event have been made tradeable

## Patch notes/Oct 13 2025
## Summary 
This update rebalances Theatre of Blood purple loot distribution to encourage grouping rather than solo MVP play, fixes Hephaestus pet animations, enables all pets to fight superior slayer creatures, and introduces Halloween theming while working on behind-the-scenes event content.

## Changes and fixes 
* TOB purple chance has been shifted around (buff)
** Instead of MVPs being heavily benefitted when it comes to rolling purples, all players get the benefit if they are 'doing their fair share' of damage
** The idea is that this will encourage players to group up because it's no longer 'optimal' to be MVP in the raid for purples (other than trying hard to do faster raids for more loot)
* Hephaestus (pet) animations have been fixed, so he doesn't just stand idle while attacking
* All pets should be able to fight superior slayer creatures now
* Depositing noted items from the looting bag should no longer put them in your bank in noted form
* The TOB scythes have now got the winners names and times baked into the examine texts

## Halloween preparations 
* Lots of behind the scenes stuff coming up for Halloween! For now it remains switched off to the public while work continues on it
* Home has received a Halloween re-theme!

## Patch notes/Oct 15 2024
## Update from Oct 15 2024 
This update brings the return of Hard Mode Zulrah ("Danger Snek") with new rewards including the Blazing Blowpipe and the Serpentine Grasp gloves. Additionally, the update introduces Olympian component conversion options, rebalances the Heavenly bow, adds the new Talaria boots, and includes various Halloween-related improvements and bug fixes.

## Hard Mode Zulrah 
Zulrah's dangerous cousin Danger Snek has made a return, and is ready to kick some asses.

Zulrah will have a chance to drop toxic orbs. Toxic orbs are used to enter the hard mode zulrah fight.

Toxic orbs can also be crafted from Zulrah's scales, or unique components.

## Rewards 
* Serpentine, Tanzanite, and Magma crests are Slayer helmet (I) upgrades that use drops from Zulrah and Danger Snek
** These provide Slayer helmet (I) slayer bonuses, and ranged/magic/melee stats at serp helm tier, respectively
* Serpentine Grasps are new ranged gloves which have a special effect that adds 15% of your max hit to your minimum hit if you are venomed
** There is also a "Self-venom" option on the Grasps which will let you enter that status effect at will for more consistently higher DPS
** The Grasps work like the Serpentine helmet, giving a chance to apply venom to your target
* New pet - Danger snek will sometimes drop a dangerous snekling, which is much much stronger than the regular Zulrah pet
* Blazing blowpipe is a new blowpipe upgrade, with higher strength bonus and lower attack bonus, requiring you to leverage attack levels and bonus from other gear in order to do high DPS against higher level mobs
** This is charged, with a separate charge pool to the other Zulrah items, and is more expensive to upkeep

## Olympian raid component changes 
There's a new vendor next to the Olympian Raid portal who will sell you Olympian Component Downgrade Tokens and Olympian Component Side-Grade Tokens.

We want to provide a way to convert some Olympian components down and sideways (i.e. not upgr

## Patch notes/Oct 20 2024
## Update from Oct 20 2024 
This update introduces a new XP locking feature that allows players to lock individual skills at their current level, enabling new account builds like pures and skillers. The update also includes various bug fixes and quality-of-life improvements such as emote unlocks and making more items tradeable.

## XP Locking 
You can now opt into locking your skill XP, at a per-skill level.

Set an unlock cost which you must pay when you no longer want it to be locked. 
* (Locking a skill is free to do)

This requires you to restart your client. If you don't see the right click option, that's what the problem is!

## Other changes 
* All (except two) emotes have been unlocked, and should now function as expected when you click them
* Cerb lava pools should no longer appear at home. If they do, they should eventually disappear
* Demonite ore no longer takes 10x longer to mine than Dragonite ore
* Halloween scythes are now tradeable
* Square sandwiches are now tradeable
* Zalcano will no longer be attackable by pets, but the max hit when standing in a blue circle has been increased by 50%
** Also, imbued tephra counts now as a 'special attack', so using the Sigil of Finality will increase your max hit from 45 to 135 when standing in a blue circle
* Zulrah attacks that hit you after Zulrah is dead, should no longer damage or venom you
* Teleporting using the August Events Plugin will no longer bug out while you're doing something like drinking from the Ornate Rejuvination Pool

## Patch notes/Oct 23 2024
## Update from Oct 23 2024 
This update primarily focuses on fixing various bugs and addressing performance issues that were causing occasional server-wide lag. Key changes include fixes for equipment visual bugs, GIM functionality improvements, and adjustments to Zulrah and TOA rewards to improve balance and accessibility.

## Group Ironman improvements 
* Swapper GIMs who are the only member left in their group, can now completely leave their group and join another Swapper GIM group
* Swapper GIMs can now leave their group without the dialog interface causing issues
* Inviting players to your group should no longer lag the world

## Performance 
* Several performance improvements have been made behind the scenes to address the occasional server-wide lag and sometimes mass disconnections

## Zulrah changes 
* The onyx has been moved to the regular table, but is still rare. Uniques table will only be the Zulrah uniques now (fangs/visage)
* Toxic orbs have been reduced from 25k scales to 15k scales to craft
* Toxic orbs now drop at 1/35 base rate instead of 1/50
* There are a couple of new achievements for Hard Mode Zulrah

## TOA changes 
* You can now break down Masori pieces into Masori crafting kits
* You can now convert Keris gems into TOA common keys

## Bug fixes 
* Luminary set proc had a bug where it would proc when you deal damage with porcupine or other special effects, but now correctly procs only on regular attacks
* Thermy pet is now in the collection log - it was meant to be there the whole time, but was excluded
* Dagannoth Prime's water surge animation has been fixed, so it'll no longer spazz out when attacking
* Volcanic heartstone (noted) is now tradeable
* When prestiging, your prayers should now get deactivated, so you can't keep higher level prayers running while you don't have the levels for it
* Many chest pieces that should remove your default arm models were bugged out, but now are fixed (e.g. justiciar chestguard)

## Patch notes/Oct 27 2025
## Summary 
This major Halloween update completely reimagines the Breaches system with player-spawned instances and structured mechanics, introduces a new active smithing system for creating event weapons with quality/heat/progress mechanics, and includes boss HP bars, ToB purple loot fixes, and several slayer-locked improvements.

## Breaches reimagined 
The breach system has been changed from last year to be more structured and manageable.

* Breaches can be spawned by players by purchasing a 'breach spawn tome' from the halloween event shop
* Breaches take place in an instance that everyone will be able to teleport to with the August Events Plugin in the client
* Teleporting into the breach will put you approximately in the center, around where the boss should spawn

Periodically, 2 things will happen in the breach:
* Big meteors will spawn over players randomly, dealing damage to players who don't dodge it in a 3x3 area
* Buffs will spawn randomly around the boss, providing different buffs that apply to you while you stand on them

You can only use event weapons or trinket weapons on breach bosses.

## Event weapons & Trinket weapons 
These weapons are designed to produce a more even playing field, and provide a catch up mechanic for newer players.

NOTE: not an even playing field, just a more even playing field. Your account progress still matters!

* Event weapons can only be used on event NPCs and breach NPCs
* Trinket weapons can be used on regular NPCs AND the aforementioned NPCs for the event

## The weapons provide the following buffs vs zombies 
* T1 event weapon: max hit of at least 35
* T2 event weapon: max hit of at least 50, with a 25% damage increase
* T3 event weapon: max hit of at least 55, with a 50% damage increase
* Trinket weapon: max hit of at least 55, with a 100% damage increase

## The weapons provide the following buffs vs breach bosses 
* T1 event weapon: max hit of at least 45
* T2 event weapon: max hit of at least 75, with a 25% damage

## Patch notes/Oct 31 2025
## Summary 
This update fixes the boss HP bar display for bosses with over 32.6k HP, adds quality-of-life improvements to the Halloween event including a smithing dialog toggle and scrap purchasing option, replaces the Coin Sacrifice animation with Cash Money, and announces new monthly voting rewards for November.

## Changes and fixes 
* The boss HP bar should no longer glitch out when bosses have over 32.6k HP, and instead will just show a percentage value for current health
* Halloween scrap has been added to the event store for those with OCD who can't stand having excess ores
* There's now a toggle on the dwarf instructor for the halloween event, allowing you to skip 1 dialog step when crafting. The second confirmation dialog still requires "Safety Off" to be skipped
* The 'Coin Sacrifice' gfx has been replaced with a cooler looking different custom graphic, and renamed to 'Cash Money'. It can now also be previewed at Hatius Cosaintus
* The 'scream' halloween pet has had its inventory options fixed so you can drop it
* The monthly voting rewards will be updated when the new month starts to give you a Breach Spawn Tome and Wicked Trinket for free!

## Patch notes/Sep 28 2025
## Summary 
This update introduces significant behind-the-scenes technical improvements to accelerate future content development, adds Tormented Demons as a new tier 100 boss with unique Demonbane weapons, and outlines upcoming content including Yama boss, achievements system upgrades, and a revamped Halloween event with improved Breaches.

## Tech upgrades 
Behind the scenes, while the League has been going on, significant technical reworking to the codebase has been undertaken (long time overdue) which should make developing custom content much faster and less difficult.

This is an investment into the future of August, and there's excitement to get cracking on some new content to take advantage of all of this, especially with all that's been learned from the league and the things that it highlighted about the game.

NOTE: This does mean that there's a pretty high chance that there will be a wide range of strange issues popping up around the game where you might not expect them. Please report these bugs so they can be fixed ASAP.

## Tormented Demons 
* Tier 100 with T100 boss level HP
* Drop new Demonbane (+40% accuracy, +30% damage) weaponry and the demonite defender
** [T98] Scorching Bow - 1 tick faster attacks vs demons, spec attack freezes demons for 20 ticks
** [T98] Emberlight - spec attack has increased damage and drains target attack/strength/defence stats
** [T98] Purging Staff - 10% (additive) increased chance to proc Elementalist (PvM talent)
** [T98] Burning Claws - chance to burn on spec, 2x "Burning claw" combined (use on each other)

All of the above new weapons do not use any charges.

## What's coming next 
* Yama boss coming to the game which will bring a whole new challenge
* Upgrade to the achievements system to make it much easier to use
* Proper boss HP bar implementation
* Picking out important bits of feedback from the community (almost-end-of-year-sweep-up)
* Preparation for another Halloween event with improved Breaches system

## Patch notes/Sep 29 2025
## Summary 
This follow-up update addresses numerous bugs introduced by the previous tech upgrades, including fixes for noted item trading, equipment swapping, collection log rendering, Apollo's models and animations, and various item interaction problems.

## Follow-up fixes 
* Noted variants of tradeable items are now also tradeable
* Equipping items properly unequips the right items accordingly. e.g. equipping a shield will unequip your 2h weapon
* Coins are tradeable again
* TDs have a collection log added, but are 0 points on the league worlds so it doesn't affect player rankings
* You can now alch noted things again
* The collection log interface on leagues worlds will properly render again
* More cases of looting noted items saying you looted a "null" have been fixed
* You'll now animate your attack when you attack a max hit dummy

* Apollo energy spawns and light elementals will now properly move as expected, instead of trying to retaliate
* Shift-click to drop on custom items should work again
* Pet rate and AFK pets should now render correctly and have the correct right-click options in your inventory

## Patch notes/Sept 05 2024
## Update from Sept 05 2024 
This major update comprehensively rebalances combat pets to align with the tier of their source boss, significantly enhances pet damage output, and adds new pet-focused talents. The update also includes several critical warnings for HCIM players about increased NPC damage, along with various quality-of-life improvements for spec switching, healing mechanics, and website functionality.

## The Great Pet Patch 
This update brings all of the combat pets in line with each other, updating their stats to reflect the tier of the boss you killed to get it.

* Barrows pets are now sitting at tier 75 (nerf)
* World boss pets at tier 90
* The new Olympian Raid pets are sitting at tiers 107, 110, and 120
* You can examine the pets to see their tiers!

Some specific notes:
* Lower tier pets have had their drop rates buffed significantly, so your grind-to-first-pet won't be as demoralizing as it has been to date
* All pets have had their damage boosted baseline by 50%
* An existing massive nerf to NPC strength has been removed. This means pets are stronger, and NPCs are stronger!
** Fair warning to all HCIM and PDHCIM!

* There are 2 new talents for pets damage:
** Pet Specialist: reduces the effect of damage mitigation against higher defence targets
** Pet Mastery: increases max hit
* Pet hitsplats will now show as a darker tint, so you can tell your hits apart from theirs
* A bunch of pets that previously couldn't properly hit Zulrah/Kree/Vorkath with ranged/mage attacks now can hit them properly (yay Guardian big buff)
* Remote recipient now works as expected

Overall, pets should feel really good, with options to spec into the utility tree to make them feel even better! There are a bunch of fixes in here for specific pets vs specific NPCs, but it should just work for you after this update.

## Patch notes/Sept 07 2024
## Update from Sept 07 2024 
This minor update improves skill guides, NPC and player following mechanics, and fixes a few bugs including essence block delivery and charge consumption issues. The update also introduces individual skill max capes for players with Prestige 7 level 126 or Prestige 8+ in their respective skills.

## Changes and improvements 
* Skill guides have been updated, and should be a better representation of what things require what stats
* Following players, and NPCs following players, should no longer have weird gaps between the two, and should be generally more responsive
* Aubury will give you noted dense essence blocks directly into your inventory again, instead of sending it to your bank, or giving UIMs a certain amount of them unnoted at a time
* Charged items should no longer lose extra charges from barrage spells and exploding attacks
* Individual skill max capes have been added for each skill, requiring P7 lvl 126, or P8+ to buy and equip

## Patch notes/Sept 09 2024
## Update from Sept 09 2024 
This update introduces a range of new benefits for donator ranks, including reduced costs for slayer task resets and talent respecs based on rank level. Dragonstone+ ranks receive exclusive perks like group proximity voted XP buffs and the ability to combine noted crystal keys. The update also adds new purchasable items, two new types of imbued hearts, and various quality-of-life improvements across the game.

## New donator rank perks 

## Slayer task reset cost - slayer points 
* Sapphire: -2 points
* Emerald: -4 points
* Ruby: -6 points
* Diamond: -8 points
* Dragonstone: -10 points
* Onyx: -13 points
* Zenyte: -16 points
* Tanzanite: -20 points
* Olympian: -25 points

## Slayer task reset cost - coins 
* Sapphire: -5%
* Emerald: -10%
* Ruby: -15%
* Diamond: -20%
* Dragonstone: -30%
* Onyx: -40%
* Zenyte: -50%
* Tanzanite: -60%
* Olympian: -70%

## Talent respec cost reduction 
Mo' respecs mo' fun!

* Sapphire: -10%
* Emerald: -15%
* Ruby: -20%
* Diamond: -30%
* Dragonstone: -40%
* Onyx: -50%
* Zenyte: -60%
* Tanzanite: -70%
* Olympian: -75%

## Dragonstone+ exclusive rewards 
Ranks up to diamond have great perks with the donator zones. I wanted to add a couple of things for the ranks afterward specifically.

## Group proximity voted XP buff 
Skip the need to be in close proximity with a friend. If you've voted in the past 12 hours, you'll get the buff!

## Combine noted crystal keys 
Skip a bit of a pain in the ass.

* Dragonstone: 5 at a time
* Onyx: 10 at a time
* Zenyte: 25 at a time
* Tanzanite: 50 at a time
* Olympian: 100 at a time

## New store items 
For those who wish to support, or continue supporting the server, there are a few new items in the store!

Each have descriptions and loot tables where applicable that are shown when you hover them.

The new items to the game that come with this are:
* The Accessory Mystery Box
* The Talent Spec Trinket
* The Arcane Grimoire

These are available in the in-game donator store for f
[... 1350 more chars ...]

## Patch notes/Sept 24 2024
## Update from Sept 24 2024 
This update includes several crafting requirement reductions, bug fixes, and quality-of-life improvements. Most notably, Zulrah now has additional attack sequences matching OSRS, the Collection Log has been cleaned up, and Dagannoth Kings drop tables have been revised to remove unwanted items. The update also hints at upcoming beta tests for TOA release and a major RuneLite update.

## Changes and improvements 
* Neitiznot faceguard now only requires 3 bandos components instead of 5
* Poseidon's olympian guardians should no longer cause a bug where Poseidon remains immune to damage
* Poseidon's trident no longer requires a kodai insignia to craft it
* Zulrah now has 3 more attack sequences, just like OSRS
* The Collection Log will no longer count 2 for items you craft instead of 1
* The Collection log also has had a bunch of old irrelevant items removed
* GIMs who want new players to join their existing group, should no longer be required to become a Swapper Group. Admins can sort this out without that being an outcome
* Zulrah's eels are now fishable again, which can be broken down into Zulrah's scales
* Dagannoth Kings have had junk removed from their drop tables - expect to see less talismans, and more rings

## Phantasm trinket
The Phantasm trinket is a item that can be opened to receive various items.

## Pickaxes
August has a number of custom pickaxes with unique perks.

## Bronze to Rune Pickaxes
Bronze through Rune pickaxes work as normal, all able to be purchased at the Skilling Tools Store at home. These tools do not have any perks.

## Shops

## Gatherer Pickaxe
50px The gatherer pickaxe has the double yield perk, providing a small chance to gather two of a resource each time you mine. It is roughly equivalent to a Rune pickaxe in speed, and has a Mining level requirement of 1.

## Shops
The gatherer pickaxe can be purchased at the Skilling Token Store at home.

## Dragon Pickaxe
50pxThe dragon pickaxe doesn't have any perks, but is a required stepping stone if looking to craft your own Pickaxes#3rd age pickaxe. It has a mining level requirement of 61. 

## Drops
The dragon pickaxe drops from the following enemies:

{| class="wikitable sortable"
! Dropped From !! Quantity !! Drop Table
|-
| Zalcano || 1 || Rare (1/64)
|-
| King Black Dragon || 1 || Rare (1/512)
|-

| Iron dragon || 1 || Super rare (1/4096)
|-
| Red dragon || 1 || Super rare (1/4096)

| Bronze dragon || 1 || Super rare (1/4096)

| Blue dragon || 1 || Super rare (1/4096)
|-
| Green dragon || 1 || Super rare (1/5120)
|}

## Infernal Pickaxe
|50pxThe infernal pickaxe has the process resource perk, and must be crafted using a smoldering stone and dragon pickaxe. It has a level requirement of 61 Mining. 

## Drops
The smoldering stone required for crafting the infernal pickaxe drops from the following enemies:

|-
| Ignis || 1 || Rare (1/42)
|-
| Hellhound || 1 || Mega Rare
|}

## Crystal Pickaxe
|50pxThe crystal pickaxe has the speed perk, and must be crafted using a crystal tool seed, crystal shards, and a dragon pickaxe. It has a level requirement of 71 Mining.

## Rainments of the Eye
The Rainments of the Eye can be purchased from the Skilling Token Store for a total of 20,000 Skilling tokens.

## Perks
The Rainments of the Eye has the following perks:

## Random event
August random events offer modest rewards in exchange for interaction within the game. Currently, there are two random events in August: the Sandwich Lady and Rick Turpentine.

Failure to interact with a random event within a minute of it spawning will result in your player being teleported to the home area. 

Players can choose to dismiss random events, however doing so does not currently count toward Achievements or High score totals.

## Rank book
50px
Not to be confused with a Bond, Rank books are non-tradeable items that can be obtained by Donating, as a rare drop from NPCs, purchased in certain shops, or won as a reward from certain events.

Rank books can redeemed towards your Donator rank but do not award Donator credits.

## Sources
For information on which NPCs can drop Rank books, see their respective pages.

## Shooting stars
thumb|A player mining a Shooting star.
Shooting stars appear randomly in the world once every hour starting at approximately 55 minutes after the hour. Stars last for 5 minutes before de-spawning.

Mining shooting stars gives Stardust which can be spent in the Stardust Store. Players can mine stars at any Mining level.

## Trivia 
Players can take damage if they stand on the landing site of a shooting star.

## Tombs of Amascut
Tombs of Amascut is one of August's raids located near the Olympian raid teleport or just north west of home. You can enter it through the Light green portal labeled Tombs of Amascut Portal.
Tombs of Amascut contains  bosses, tier 115, Ba-Ba, Zebak, Akkha, Kephri, and tier 120 bosses The Wardens.

You must defeat each boss to get their respective File:toa key fragment (baba).png key fragment to combine for the File:toa rare key.png TOA Rare key.This key contains Toombs of Amascut's best items such as the File:Tumeken's shadow.png Tumeken's shadow, File:Osmumten's fang.png Osmumten's fang, File:Elidinis' ward.png Elidinis' ward, and the File:Masori body (f).png Masori's set.
Category:Todo
Category:Raids
Category:todo-contributor

## Torva armour
File:Torva full helm.png earth

Torva armour is a set of armour requiring X Levels to equip. Torva armour is a set of equipment requiring level xx xxxx to equip. The individual armour pieces are obtainable only as rewards from Nex.

The pieces of armour are dropped in a broken state, and must be repaired using 

## Set Bonus
While the full set is equipped, you have a 20% increased chance (multiplicative) to proc the Feral Fighter talent.

## Set Items

Torva full helm
Torva platebody
Torva platelegs

## Vote Obelisk
The Vote Obelisk is accessible to all players.

The Vote Obelisk can be used to redeem Monthly Vote Rewards and spend Vote Streak Points.

## Monthly Vote Rewards 
The monthly vote rewards offers 25 rewards each month. These rewards reset on the 1st of the month and can change each month. To gain Votes This Month, you must claim your votes in-game (available every 12 hours).

Example Vote Rewards from May 2025

702x702px

## Vote Streak Store 

The shop uses vote streak points as its currency. Vote streak points are accumulated every 7 days of voting specifcally on RULOCUS:

7 days = 1 point

14 days = 2 points

21/28/35/42/etc... days = 3 points

{| class="wikitable sortable"

Category:Shops

## August token
50px The August token is a tradeable item. When used, it awards donator points which can be spent at the Donator store. August tokens and points can be transferred too and from at a 1:1 ratio.

## Combat xp token
## Major combat xp token

50px The Major combat xp token is a tradeable item received from voting. When used, it provided the player a 20% xp boost to combat experience for 2 hours.

## Minor combat xp token

50px The Minor combat xp token is a tradeable item received from voting. When used, it provided the player a 20% xp boost to combat experience for 30 minutes.

## PVM token
50px|leftPVM tokens are a currency dropped by performing skilling actions in August. Skilling tokens can be spent at the PVM Token Store for various upgrades and rewards. 

PVM tokens are tradeable.

## Skilling xp token
## Major skilling xp token

50px The Major skilling xp token is a tradeable item received from voting. When used, it provided the player a 20% xp boost to skilling experience for 2 hours.

## Minor skilling xp token

50px The Minor skilling xp token is a tradeable item received from voting. When used, it provided the player a 20% xp boost to skilling experience for 30 minutes.

## Vote token
50px The Vote token is a tradeable item received from voting. When used, it awards vote points which can be spent at the Vote store. Vote tokens and points can be transferred too and from at a 1:1 ratio.

# CATEGORY: SKILLS (79 pages)
############################################################

## Arcane prayer scroll
50px|left Reading an Arcane prayer scroll allows the player to unlock Augury, which requires 70 Defence and 77 Prayer. There are no skill requirements to read the scroll, but reading it will consume it.

## Bottomless Prayer Renewal
50px|left The Bottomless Prayer Renewal is a bottomless version of the Prayer enhance potion. The Bottomless Prayer Renewal boosts the player's prayer point regeneration by 1 prayer point every 10 ticks (6s). 

This stat boost is applied for 5 minutes until the effect wears off. The Bottomless Prayer Renewal is tradeable.

## Cerberite slayer helmet
left|64px
The Cerberite slayer helmet is a tier 100 helmet.

## Combat stats

## Passive Effect
While equipped, provides a 15.6% increase to slayer accuracy and a 17.3% increase to slayer damage (identical to the Slayer helmet (i)). In addition to this, a flat +15 damage bonus is provided against demons.

## Cooking
Cooking is a trainable skill in August. 

Category:Skills

## Cooking/Guide
50pxThis is a guide for training the Cooking skill. This guide has a basic outline of methods to train the skill, and which Talents will impact the skill to aide in your training.

This guide does not promise to be the most efficient, best xp/hour, or best money-making guide. Rather, it is a good option for those looking for help deciding how to train their skills.

## Training

## Level 1-70
Players starting Cooking training should cook the best available fish they have, up until level 70 for sharks.

## Level 70+ - Sharks/Minnows
This section assumes that the fishing guide was followed.
Starting at level 70, players can begin cooking sharks or minnows.

## Prestige 1, Level 80+
Starting at Prestige 1, level 80, players can begin cooking marinated fish at the Olympian brazier. Marinating fish requires Honey. There are a total of 3 fish to marinate.

## Bonuses

## Talent Synergies

## Crafting
Crafting is a trainable skill in August. 

Category:Skills

## Crafting/Guide
50pxThis is a guide for training the Crafting skill. This guide has a basic outline of methods to train the skill, and which Talents will impact the skill to aide in your training.

This guide does not promise to be the most efficient, best xp/hour, or best money-making guide. Rather, it is a good option for those looking for help deciding how to train their skills.

## Training

## Level 1-20 - Leather
Players starting Crafting should make leather items until level 20.

## Level 20+ - Gems
Cutting gems starting at level 20 is a great way to level up [[crafting] quickly, and can be paired well with the Thieving guide to collect a large amount of gems and also gain xp in Thieving

## Level 45+ - Dragon leather
Crafting dragon leather is an altnernative method to cutting gems which will also net you a large amount of xp/hour.

## Talent Synergies

Farming is a trainable skill in August.

## Farming/Guide
50pxThis is a guide for training the Farming skill. This guide has a basic outline of methods to train the skill, and which Talents will impact the skill to aide in your training.

This guide does not promise to be the most efficient, best xp/hour, or best money-making guide. Rather, it is a good option for those looking for help deciding how to train their skills.

## Training

## Level 1+ - Planting Allotment/Herb Seeds
Training Farming can be done by planting seeds in the allotment and herb patches located east of home. Players are recommended to plant the highest level seeds they can in all of the available farming patches.

An easy way to obtain seeds is to pickpocket the master farmer that is found roaming around the farming patches.

## Bonuses and Talents

## Bonuses

## Talent Synergies

Category:Guide

The "Farming goodies" shop is accessible to Ironman players.

The shop uses coins as its currency.

## Firemaking
Firemaking is a trainable skill in August. 

## Firemaking Resources

Category:Skills

## Firemaking/Guide
50pxThis is a guide for training the Firemaking skill. This guide has a basic outline of methods to train the skill, and which Talents will impact the skill to aide in your training.

This guide does not promise to be the most efficient, best xp/hour, or best money-making guide. Rather, it is a good option for those looking for help deciding how to train their skills.

## Training
Training Firemaking is recommended after players have unlocked the Bonfire talent, as this significantly reduces the click intensity and greatly increases the XP/hour.

Players should use the highest level logs they can on the fire for maximum XP/hour.

## Bonuses and Talents

## Talent Synergies

## Fishing
Fishing is a trainable skill in August. 

## Fishing Resources

Category:Skills

## Fishing/Guide
50pxThis is a guide for training the Fishing skill. This guide has a basic outline of methods to train the skill, and which Talents will impact the skill to aide in your training.

This guide does not promise to be the most efficient, best xp/hour, or best money-making guide. Rather, it is a good option for those looking for help deciding how to train their skills.

## Training

## Level 1-76 - Best Available
Players starting Fishing training should fish from the best available fishing spot they can catch from, up until level 76 for sharks.

## Level 76+ - Sharks
Starting with sharks at level 76, players can continue this path until 99 and beyond.

## Alternate: Level 80+ - Olympian Fishing
Starting at level 80, players can begin fishing big bass at Olympian fishing spots. There are a total of 3 fish to catch at Olympian fishing locations. Each subsequent fish requires an additional prestige to catch.

## Alternate: Level 82+ - Minnows
Starting at level 82, players can begin fishing minnows. While not the best fishing xp, minnows provide very good cooking xp.

## Bonuses and Talents

## Bonuses

## Talent Synergies

Category:Guide

## Fishing tools
August has a number of custom fishing tools with unique perks.

## Standard Fishing Tools
The standard fishing tools work as normal, and all able to be purchased at the Skilling Tools Store at home. These tools do not have any perks.

## Shops

## Pearl Fishing Tools
50pxPearl fishing tools all have the speed perk, and are used in place of regular Fishing tools. They cannot be equipped. 

The Molch pearls required for crafting the pearl fishing tools can be purchased from the Skilling Token Store.

## Recipes

## Dragon Harpoon
50pxThe dragon harpoon doesn't have any perks, but is a required stepping stone if looking to craft an infernal harpoon. It has a Fishing level requirement of 61, and can be equipped. 

## Drops
The dragon harpoon drops from the following enemies:
{| class="wikitable sortable"
! Source !! Quantity !! Drop Table
|-
| Alchemical Hydra || 1 || Uniques
|-
| King Black Dragon || 1 || Rare (1/512)
|-
| Shadow Wyrm || 1 || Rare (1/256)
|-
| Steel dragon || 1 || Super rare (1/4096)
|-
| Black dragon || 1 || Super rare (1/4096)
|-
| Iron dragon || 1 || Super rare (1/4096)
|-
| Red dragon || 1 || Super rare (1/4096)
|-
| Bronze dragon || 1 || Super rare (1/4096)
|-
| Blue dragon || 1 || Super rare (1/4096)
|-
| Wyrm || 1 || Rare (1/256)
|-
| Green dragon || 1 || Super rare (1/5120)
|}

## Infernal Harpoon
The infernal harpoon has the process resource perk, and must be crafted using a smouldering stone and dragon harpoon. It has a Fishing level requirement of 61, and can be equipped. 

## Drops
The smouldering stone required for crafting the infernal harpoon drops from the following enemies:
{| class="wikitable sortable"
! Source !! Quantity !! Drop Table
|-
| Cerberus || 1 || Rare (1/64)
|-
| Ignis || 1 || Rare (1/42)
|-
| Hellhound || 1 || Mega Rare
|}

## Recipe

## Crystal Harpoon
|50pxThe crystal harpoon has the speed perk, and must be crafted using a crystal tool seed, crystal shards, and a Dragon harpoon. It has a level requirement of 71 Fishing.

## Fletching
Fletching is a trainable skill in August. 

Category:Skills

## Fletching/Guide
50pxThis is a guide for training the Fletching skill. This guide has a basic outline of methods to train the skill, and which Talents will impact the skill to aide in your training.

This guide does not promise to be the most efficient, best xp/hour, or best money-making guide. Rather, it is a good option for those looking for help deciding how to train their skills.

## Training

## Level 1+
Players starting Fletching should start with bolts. This method can be paired well with the smithing guide to collect a large amount of unfinished bolts while training Smithing.

## Level 70+ - Amethyst Darts
A popular alternative to bolts is to fletch amethyst into dart tips, and then those dart tips into darts.

## P1 Level 80+: Dragonstone Donator Zone
If the player is a Dragonstone Donator, you can take advantage of the Scrapey Tree at Prestige 1 level 80 Fletching in the Dragonstone Donator Zone.

## Bonuses and Talents

## Bonuses

## Talent Synergies

## Game modes/slayer locked faq
= Slayer Locked Frequently Asked Questions =

## General

## What about X item that doesn't drop from any Slayer mobs?
New  Slayer tasks have been implemented to ensure that items can be obtained on a Slayer Locked account. 
Some items may remain unobtainable, if it seems like it is not a fitting addition in the first place.
e.g. Farmers dropping  farming tools makes sense. Dragons dropping  amulets of strength do not. 

## What skills are locked for Slayer Locked accounts?
The following skills cannot be trained by a Slayer Locked account, in any capacity

## Can I attack Treasure Goblins? There are no tasks for them?
Yes.

## Can you complete Tombs of Amascut (ToA), Olympian Raids, Nex, and Hardmode boss variants as a Slayer Locked account?
Yes, you will be able to complete this content with the required Slayer Prestige:
* Prestige 1 Slayer:  Olympian Raids (unlocks task)
* Prestige 2:  ToA (unlocks task)
* Prestige 3:  Nex &  Azrael (unlocks task)
* Prestige 4:  Ignis
* Prestige 5:  Danger Snek

## Will Slayer Tasks be added for Tombs of Amascut (ToA), Olympian Raids, and Nex?
Yes, but the  slayer helm bonuses do not work for these tasks. 

## Who can I party up with on a Slayer Locked account?
Slayer Locked players can ONLY party with other Slayer Locked players.
For the release competition, this will be further restricted to only being able to party with players in the same competition bracket as you.
If you are still qualified to compete, but you no longer want to compete so that your restrictions can be lifted, create a ticket in Discord and provide information for staff to be able to manually disqualify you.

## What are the biggest advantages to playing a Slayer Locked mode? 
* Gaining 5% additional drop rate vs bosses, PER  Slayer prestige rank.
* Having a  Prestige Cap of 9
* Gaining access to all tiers of the  Skilling Talent Tree without needing to fulfil requirements for 'x points in previous tiers'.
* 2x Superior Slayer spawn rate, which stacks with ot
[... 1568 more chars ...]

## Herblore
Herblore is a trainable skill in August. 

## Herblore Resources

Category:Skills

## Herblore/Guide
50pxThis is a guide for training the Herblore skill. This guide has a basic outline of methods to train the skill, and which Talents will impact the skill to aide in your training.

This guide does not promise to be the most efficient, best xp/hour, or best money-making guide. Rather, it is a good option for those looking for help deciding how to train their skills.

## Training

## Level 1+ - Best Available
Players starting Herblore should create the best potion that is available to them. This process can be repeated until level 99 and beyond.

## Alternate: Level 45-90 - Overload Prep
Starting at Level 45, players can begin creating "super sets", a prerequisite to creating super combat potions. A popular item, the Bottomless Overload, requires super combat potions in it's creation.

## Level 90+ - Super Combat
Starting at level 90, players can create super combat potions. A popular item, the Bottomless Overload, requires super combat potions in it's creation.

## Bonuses

## Talent Synergies

Category:Skills
Category:Guide

## Mining
Mining is a trainable skill in August. 

## Mining Resources

Category:Skills

## Mining/Guide
50pxThis is a guide for training the Mining skill. This guide has a basic outline of methods to train the skill, and which Talents will impact the skill to aide in your training.

This guide does not promise to be the most efficient, best xp/hour, or best money-making guide. Rather, it is a good option for those looking for help deciding how to train their skills.

## Training

## Level 1+ - Shooting Stars
Starting at level 1, players can mine the Shooting Star event. The shooting star offers a non click-intensive training method that also awards stardust which can be sold at the Stardust Store

## Alternate: Level 1-50 - Best Available
Players starting Mining should mine the best node that is available to them. This process can be repeated until level 99 and beyond.

Mining Iron until level 50 is also a good option.

## Alternate: Level 50+ - Granite
Mining Granite is recommended for fast XP. Mining granite will give ganite dust as well as granite (5kg), which are both used in creating granite armor, which is a method listed in the Smithing guide.

## Bonuses and Talents

## Bonuses

## Talent Synergies

## Prayer
Prayer is a trainable skill in August. 

## Prayer Resources

Category:Skills

## Prayer/Guide
50pxThis is a guide for training the Prayer skill. This guide has a basic outline of methods to train the skill, and which Talents will impact the skill to aide in your training.

This guide does not promise to be the most efficient, best xp/hour, or best money-making guide. Rather, it is a good option for those looking for help deciding how to train their skills.

## Training

## Burying Bones
Bury bones for Prayer experience.

The Boner Boost talent will boost this method.

## Altar Offering
If you don't want to bury bones, you can also offer them to the altar at home. Ensouled heads can be offered to the altar as well.

The Devout talent will boost this method.

## Bonecrusher
If you don't want to do either of those options, you can use a Bonecrusher as well.

The Bone Crusher++ talent will boost this method.

## Bonuses and Talents

## Bonuses

## Talent Synergies

## Runecrafting
Runecrafting is a trainable skill in August. 

## Runecrafting Resources

Category:Skills

## Runecrafting/Guide
50pxThis is a guide for training the Runecrafting skill. This guide has a basic outline of methods to train the skill, and which Talents will impact the skill to aide in your training.

This guide does not promise to be the most efficient, best xp/hour, or best money-making guide. Rather, it is a good option for those looking for help deciding how to train their skills.

## Setup
In order to start Runecrafting, players will need essence. In August, Dense essence blocks are used as the primary essence instead of rune or pure essence. Essence can be mined by teleporting via Aubury at Home, or obtained by killing various bosses.

Dense essence blocks are the primary essence you will use in August, and are used directly on the Runecrafting altar. Players may also receive Essence shards. 

Essence shards can be exchanged at Aubury for Dense essence blocks at a 1:1 ratio. (100 shards turned in gives 100 essence blocks).

## Training

## Level 1+
Once you have collected your Dense essence blocks, players can teleport to the Runecrafting altar by selecting the runecrafting teleport on the Portal Nexus at home. The default option, bind random is recommended when Runecrafting. This method should be used for the entirety of your training.

Important note: The Banker's Note Talent does not work on essence blocks!

## Bonuses and Talents

## Bonuses

## Talent Synergies
Talents marked with a * will only affect essence collection!

Category:Skills
Category:Guide

## Slayer
Slayer is a trainable skill in August. 

Category:Skills

## Slayer/Guide
50pxThis is a guide for training the Slayer skill. This guide has a basic outline of methods to train the skill, and which Talents will impact the skill to aide in your training.

This guide does not promise to be the most efficient, best xp/hour, or best money-making guide. Rather, it is a good option for those looking for help deciding how to train their skills.

## Training

## Level 1+
In general, you should obtain tasks from the highest level Slayer master that your Combat and Slayer levels allow. The monsters they assign generally give more experience than lower level Slayer masters. Nixite is the best Slayer master to use for the fastest experience, but some players may find that Nieve's tasks better suit their preferences. 
Note that just because the player can access better Slayer masters, this does not directly translate to more experience per hour. This is especially true for Slayer Locked accounts.

## Post-Prestige Early Levels
A very specific "meta" method of leveling Slayer again after you Prestige is to create Slayer mystery boxes. When crafted, they give a chunk of Slayer XP that helps skip the early levels.

## Tasks
Slayer masters assign tasks based on your Slayer level and not on your Combat level. Players should take this into account when picking Slayer tasks, especially at early combat levels.

Some Slayer tasks will be restricted until certain Slayer Prestiges.

There are currently 4 Slayer masters to receive tasks from, with varying Slayer level requirements.
* For a list of all tasks from each Slayer master, go to their pages.

## Slayer Items
A full list of items that affect Slayer are available in the Slayer Point Store shop, but the following list are the most impactful.

Black Mask, Slayer helmet and Slayer helm (i) all provide damage bonuses to every combat style.

Note that special slayer items such as mirror shields, nose pegs, earmuffs, etc. are not required to kill slayer NPCs on August.

## Slayer Points/Streak Bonus

## Slayer helmet
48px|left Slayer helmet provides a stronger damage boost than the Black mask to all 3 combat styles when worn versus your current slayer task.

## Combat Stats
 

## Special Passive Effect
Slayer helmet provides a 9.1% increase to slayer accuracy and a 11.15% increase to slayer strength with all styles of combat when worn and attacking your assigned slayer task.This can be further increased with the Tier 4 Skilling Talent 20pxPointy Helmet.

## Smithing
Smithing is a trainable skill in August. 

## Smithing Resources
The following table is an outline of all available bars that can be smithed in August. For a comprehensive list of all available recipes, see Smithing Recipes

Category:Skills

## Smithing/Guide
50pxThis is a guide for training the Smithing skill. This guide has a basic outline of methods to train the skill, and which Talents will impact the skill to aide in your training.

This guide does not promise to be the most efficient, best xp/hour, or best money-making guide. Rather, it is a good option for those looking for help deciding how to train their skills.

## Training

## Level 1+ - Unfinished Bolts
This guide assumes you are smelting your own bars.

Smithing bars into unfinished bolts is a good multi-skill method, as darts are a fast method to gain Fletching xp, as referenced in the fletching guide

## Level 50+ - Granite Armor
Smithing granite armor is an alternative option to smithing bolts. If the mining guide was followed, you will have collected a fair amount of granite dust as well as granite, both of which are used in creating granite armor.

## Bonuses and Talents

## Bonuses

## Talent Synergies

## Talent guides/PvM/Magic early game
This build uses a total of 42 PvM talent points, and aims to provide reasonably efficient early-game talent choices.

If you would rather have the ability to choose slayer tasks over receiving Treasure Goblins, pick Unnatural Selection over Treasure Goblins.
* If you are worried about using runes too quickly, you can pick Runic Efficiency over Treasure Goblins.

You can alternatively max out the Menacing Mage talent rather than Consistency if you want a higher maximum potential DPS with, rather than more consistent DPS.

## Talent build 

Category: Talent builds

## Talent guides/PvM/Magic mid game
This build uses 135 talent points to maximize DPS for no-damage encounters and Slayer, ideal for bosses like the Giant mole and Thermonuclear smoke devil.

An additional 15 points can be spent to spec into Runic Efficiency to reduce the number of runes used during casting by 75%.

You rely on your HP being low (using a Dwarven rock cake) to trigger Berserker’s DPS boost while Aggression and Precision help to exploit enemy weaknesses for extra damage.

Renewal and Holey Vestments provide near-infinite prayer regeneration, though some gear with a prayer bonus is needed to fully sustain an overhead prayer.

When your enemy has no weaknesses, Unnatural Selection and Firebender are good alternatives to meet the Tier 5 point requirement.

Alternatively, if you're concerned about taking damage or don't want to use a Dwarven rock cake, you can replace your Tier 5 pick with Guardian or Executioner.

## Talent build 

Category: Talent builds

## Talent guides/PvM/Ranged early game
This build uses a total of 42 PvM talent points, and aims to provide reasonably efficient early-game talent choices.

If you would rather have the ability to choose slayer tasks over receiving Treasure Goblins, pick Unnatural Selection over Treasure Goblins.
* If you are worried about using arrows/darts/bolts too quickly, you can pick Bottomless Quiver over Treasure Goblins.

You can alternatively max out the Ruthless Ranger talent rather than Consistency if you want a higher maximum potential DPS with, rather than more consistent DPS.

## Talent build 

Category: Talent builds

## Talent guides/PvM/Ranged late game defensive
This PvM build uses 282 talent points to maximize defensive talents, while still providing solid DPS.

You can swap the Tier 5 talents Guardian and Executioner as needed; Guardian provides consistent DPS, while Executioner offers a large burst to execute high-health enemies. 

For increased damage reduction, replace your Tier 5 pick with Absolute Unit.

## Talent build

This build uses 135 talent points to maximize DPS for no-damage encounters and Slayer, ideal for bosses like the Giant mole and Thermonuclear smoke devil.

You rely on your HP being low (using a Dwarven rock cake) to trigger Berserker’s DPS boost while Aggression and Precision help to exploit enemy weaknesses for extra damage.

Renewal and Holey Vestments provide near-infinite prayer regeneration, though some gear with a prayer bonus is needed to fully sustain an overhead prayer.

When your enemy has no weaknesses, Unnatural Selection and Firebender are good alternatives to meet the Tier 5 point requirement.

Alternatively, if you're concerned about taking damage or don't want to use a Dwarven rock cake, you can replace your Tier 5 pick with Guardian or Executioner.

## Thieving/Guide
50pxThis is a guide for training the Thieving skill. This guide has a basic outline of methods to train the skill, and which Talents will impact the skill to aide in your training.

This guide does not promise to be the most efficient, best xp/hour, or best money-making guide. Rather, it is a good option for those looking for help deciding how to train their skills.

## Training

## Level 1+ - Stalls
For the purpose of this guide we will assume the player does not have a donator rank and subsequent access to donator zones

Players starting Thieving should begin by stealing from the best available thieving stall they can. Staying at a specific stall such as the gem stalls can be beneficial if following the crafting guide.

## Alternate: Level 80+ - Honey
Stealing honey from beehives is an alternate method that has the added beenfit of being used for olympian cooking recipes, as outlined in the cooking guide.

## Woodcutting/Guide
50pxThis is a guide for training the Woodcutting skill. This guide has a basic outline of methods to train the skill, and which Talents will impact the skill to aide in your training.

This guide does not promise to be the most efficient, best xp/hour, or best money-making guide. Rather, it is a good option for those looking for help deciding how to train their skills.

## Training

## Level 1+ - Cutting Trees
Players are recommended to chop the highest level tree they can. 

This guide is assuming the player has the Masterful Gatherer talent. This talent makes it worth it to chop the highest level tree a player can, despite the increased time to gather.

## Anniversary League
right|700px

## Important Dates/Times

## Start Time
*The August Anniversary League will begin on: Saturday, July 26, 2025: 9:00PM
**Times listed below are in UTC-0. Please convert these accordingly to your local time zone.

## GIM Preparation
In order to give Group Ironmen time to create their groups, the League worlds will open 24 hours ahead of time, locked to the tutorial only, to allow for group creation/invitation.
*The August Anniversary League GIM Preparation will begin on: Friday, July 25, 2025: 9:00PM
**Times listed below are in UTC-0. Please convert these accordingly to your local time zone.

## End Time
*The August Anniversary League will close on Sunday, October 5, 2025: 9:00PM
**Times listed below are in UTC-0. Please convert these accordingly to your local time zone.

## Overview
The standard RSPS Leagues experience didn't feel right for with August, with the whole "milk a cow, get 10 points", "pick an onion in Lumbridge, get 10 points" thing. So, it will work like this:

*You will gain League Points for every collection log slot filled.
**Getting 1 Dharok Platebody will give you points, but only the first time.
*There will be a large amount of easy collection log slots to hunt down, and they will get more difficult as you go.
**Tip: 'Skilling Resources' has its own massive log to fill with every gatherable resource.
*As you gain points, you will fill a progress bar on the new Anniversary League Perks interface.
*As you reach certain point thresholds, you will unlock a Relic slot, with a choice between 3 different perks.
*More points are required between each tier, but the perks will also greatly increase your player's abilities to progress in the game.
** All existing August talents are all available, and work the same as before! The Anniversary League relics are another layer on top to make you stronger. Lots of the Anniversary League relics actually enhance the existing talents!

## Rules

## Player Restrictions
*All players will start as an Ironma
[... 10923 more chars ...]

## Donating
50px Donating is the primary method of supporting the development of August RSPS. 

To donate, type ::donate in-game to open the store.
*Donations are automatically delivered to a player's mailbox. Mailboxes can be found around the Home area of August, and rewards will be available for claim immediately after purchase.

## Donation Information
right|650px

## Payment Methods
August RSPS accepts the following methods of payment for any donations:

    Credit/Debit Card: These payments are made via the August RSPS website. We utilize Stripe for all payments. Stripe supports Apple and Google Pay, along with a number of local payment processors depending on your location.
    OSRS Gold: These payments are accepted through the August Discord.

Players will gain different Donator Ranks at different total donated values. To see perks and the total donation requirements for each rank, please review the Donator Rank Table.

## Donator Credits
Donator Credits are gained when redeeming Bonds. These credits be spent in the Donator store or the Donator cosmetic store for various items.

## Donator Rank
Donator Rank is gained by redeeming Bonds, Rank books, or is awarded at a 1:1 ratio for every dollar spent on the store.
*If you donate $10, you will receive 10 Donator Rank.
*If you're looking to maximize your Donator Rank, you can "double dip" by purchasing a Rank book utilizing the Donator Credits received from redeeming a Bond.
**Example: If you purchase a $25 Bond and redeem it, you can then spend the 25 Donator Credits on a Rank book to increase your total Donator Rank to 50

## Donating/Rank transfers
Players can transfer their Donator rank to other accounts that they own by speaking to Hatius Cosaintus at Home.

## Drop rate
The Drop rate is the frequency at which an NPC, chest, or other lootable source is expected to yield a certain item when killed or looted by the player.

For a more in-depth analysis of specific elements like Weighting and Grouping, see the Loot mechanics page.

## Identifying Drop Rate
In August, most drop rates can be identified by either searching the Wiki, or right-click examine the NPC in-game that you are looking to identify.

When viewing either of these sources, you may see that the drop rate is expressed as "Common" or "Rare", instead of with a specific number. 
* Note that this is intentional and means the exact rate is being obfuscated. 

## Probability
Drop rates such as "1 in 5" do not guarantee such a drop will be obtained after 5 kills. Instead, it can be thought that on average, a player would receive the drop once per 5 kills, with the chance to receive more or less.

Important note: This section does not take into account drop rate Weighting, so please review this page accordingly to verify your understanding.

## Game modes
=Game Modes=
August has 4 different game modes to choose from:

## Skiller Game Mode
Left|50px Choose this mode if you plan to focus primarily on Skilling.

* The Skiller game mode has a 20x multiplier for all XP gains.
* The Skiller game mode gains 20% more Talent points in non-combat skills.
* Max Prestige in any trainable skill is 7.

## PvMer Game Mode
Left|50px Choose this mode if you plan to focus primarily on PvM.

* The PvMer game mode has a 20x multiplier for all XP gains.
* The PvMer game mode earns 20% more Talent Points in combat skills.
* Max Prestige in any trainable skill is 7

## Masochist Game Mode
Left|50px Choose this mode if only you want to experience the game in a more challenging way.

* The Masochist game has a 5x multiplier for all XP gains.
* The Masochist game mode earns 10% more Talent Points in all skills.
* Max Prestige in any trainable skill is 10

## Slayer Locked Game Mode
Left|50px Test your game knowledge, limiting yourself to only killing NPCs on Slayer tasks!

## Slayer Locked Features
* The Slayer Locked game mode has a 10x multiplier for all XP gains.
* The Slayer Locked game mode earns 20% more Talent points in combat skills.
* The max prestige in any trainable skill is 9.
* Superior monster variants spawn 2x more often.
* 5% extra drop rate vs bosses per Slayer prestige rank, for a maximum of 45% increased drop rate.
* Every claimed Runelocus vote awards an extra talent point orb, up to 100 times.
* Access to Talents of all tiers in the Skilling talent tree without needing to unlock prior amount of talents in previous tiers.
** i.e. You can spend 4 of your first Skilling Talent Points unlocking Boss Slayer, a Tier 4 talent.

## End-game unlocks
Slayer Locked accounts gain access to the following instances at various Slayer prestiges:
* Prestige 1:  Olympian Raids (unlocks task)
* Prestige 2:  ToA (unlocks task)
* Prestige 3:  Nex &  Azrael (unlocks task)
* Prestige 4:  Ignis
* Prestige 5:  Danger Snek
Accessible shops:
* Slay
[... 1866 more chars ...]

## Ironman modes
= Ironman Modes =
There are seven types of ironman accounts:

## Generic Ironman Mechanics 

## Ironman Rules/Restrictions 
* Ironmen cannot use the Grand Exchange.
* Ironmen can only used specific shops in the  home area.

## Non-Group Ironman Rules/Restrictions 
* Non-group ironmen must do at least 15% of the damage to a boss to receive loot.
* Non-group ironmen cannot trade any other player.
* Non-group ironmen CAN join instances with other ironmen and non-ironman accounts.

## Permadeath Hardcore Ironman Mechanics 

## Permadeath Hardcore Ironman Rules/Restrictions 
* 5% increased droprate (no other account type receives any inherent droprate bonus)
* Permadeath accounts lose account progress on death, being sent back to the tutorial.

## Progress lost on death 
* All tradeable items (including those donated for)
* All skills and prestiges
* All equipped items and items in transmog slots that are tradeable

## Group Ironman  

## Group Ironman Rules/Restrictions 
* Group ironmen must have their group deal at least 90% of the damage to an enemy to get loot.
* Group ironmen can trade within their group, and have access to a group bank.
* Adding a new member to a group ironman group requires the group downgrade to a swapper group.
** Unless new member is a fresh group ironman off tutorial.

## De-ironing and Changing Ironman Modes 
De-ironing or changing ironman modes can be done by speaking to the Ironman Tutor at  home. All requests for de-ironing from a group or modifying group ironman membership must be submitted through a ticket on Discord.

The following chart illustrates the available ironman mode swaps:
500px

Category:Guides

## Item sacrifice
In order to sacrifice items, one must have items.

## Getting There
Using the ::afk command, you will see it just north east of the barrier.

## Item Sacrificing
By clicking Sacrifice on the bench you can see current promotional items. These items will give bonus points when sacrificed. Not every item is able to be sacrificed, this content is aimed toward mid-end game players.

## Pantheon League
=Overview=
right|500px|thumb|The August Pantheon League beings May 30th!

The August Pantheon League is a temporary game mode that starts on May 30, 2026. Temporary accounts progresses in 3 designated Leagues worlds, separate from the main game. Players begin as an Ironman character and unlock buffs in the form of Relics and Divine Pacts to further bolster progress.

Start time conversions:

17:00 EDT (UTC-4) | 21:00 UTC (UTC-0) | 9:00 NZST (UTC +12)

=Rules=

## Changes from Main Game
*Access to the existing Talents are disabled - Relics, Relic Tier Passives, and Divine Pacts will take their place

## Player Restrictions
Players will be an Ironman. Players will start on a fresh League account - Standard game modes such as PvM, Skiller, Masochist, and Slayer Locked are not available in the League.

## Group Ironman Changes
Group Ironman is available for the Pantheon League. However, groups will be locked once created.
*Players are NOT able to leave a group once the group is finalized.
*Groups CAN add new accounts to their group if they have an open slot, but only if account has yet to complete the tutorial.
**Existing accounts cannot be added to a Group Ironman after the fact,  known as Swapper GIM.
*NO EXCEPTIONS will be made to this rule.

## Other Restrictions
Shop restrictions for Ironmen accounts are lifted in the League worlds.

## Donations
*Any donations made via Stripe on your League account will award Rank books being automatically added to your main-game account.
*Any items donated for on the League worlds will not be transferred back to the main-game.
*Donator Rank obtained not via donating will not be transferred back to your main account.
* Rank transferring to, from, or in-between League accounts will not be offered.

=Divine Pacts=
Main Article: Pantheon League/Divine Pacts

Divine Pacts are a complementary system to relics that focuses on providing powerful and unique combat boosts. Pacts will work similar to the main-game Talents system, but progre
[... 8163 more chars ...]

## Pantheon League/Achievements
Achievements will be shown once all relevant data is revealed.

## Pantheon League/Divine Pacts
Divine Pacts are a complementary system to relics that focuses on providing powerful and unique combat boosts. Pacts will work similar to the main-game Talents system, but progression is less linear and has many more options.

There are over 200 individual nodes in the pact tree which are allocated with Pact Points. A total of 100 Pact Points are available (only up to 75 can be spent).

The Divine Pact tree can be completely reset up to [REDACTED] times.

Click here to use the Divine Pacts planner to plan and visualize your route
=Divine Pacts tasks=
The following tasks unlock Divine Pact points when completed. A total of 100 Pact Points are available (only up to 75 can be spent) along with [REDACTED] full tree resets.

Divine Pact tasks can be found under the Achievements menu in-game.

## Starter Tasks

## Paragon League
500px|right

=Overview=
The August Paragon League was a temporary game mode that ended on April 15, 2026. Temporary accounts progresseds in 3 designated Leagues worlds, separate from the main game. Players began as an Ironman character and unlocked buffs in the form of Relics and Paragon Points to further bolster progress.

## Changes from Anniversary League

*Access to the existing Talents and Pandora's Perks are disabled, with Relics offering even more power than the previous Anniversary League!
*The previous Fortune Relic has been removed.
* A Random Event mystery box has been added, which will roll the random event unique items to help with completing that collection log. You'll be able to trade in Clue uniques for tokens, and tokens can buy the boxes.
* The Leagues 'extra area teleporter' that gives you access to instances with more NPC spawns, now has a Saradomin GWD option, adding 2 extra instances per world, at a cost of 2m coins per entry.
* The Fight Caves instance will now no longer boot you from the instance after the fight. If some extra loot drops, or your inventory is full, you can stay and pick it up before leaving when you're ready.
* The League tier unlock speedrun leaderboards now have separate sections for GIM / non-GIM

## Clue Scroll System
Acquire clues by mining/fishing/woodcutting/pvming, and complete them over time for chances at unique loot! The Clue Scroll system is a brand new addition, bringing a taste of the Idle Game genre, allowing you to passively complete Clue Scrolls while you're gaming. There are 6 Clue Slots which can be unlocked with 'clue points', which you get from completing Clue Scrolls!
