## Paragon League/Guide
## Paragon League Tier 2 Rush Guide 
The goal of this guide is to provide a simple step-by-step path to unlocking your Tier 1 and Tier 2 Relics, regardless of player skill level and with as little dependence on RNG as possible. 

This is not the only way to reach Tier 2. This guide does not promise to be the fastest or most optimal method to Tier 2.

Other methods do exist and may be faster, but this guide can be completed by anyone. This guide begins after completion of tutorial island and once you find yourself at home.

## Tips 
*VOTE! VOTE! VOTE! - Voting rewards and buffs help progression immensely!

*Go to every Vote Boss and World Boss - Various achievements, high value collection logs, and XP for combat training are available here

*Interact with every random event - Random event rewards will fill in collection log slots, increasing points!

*Answer Trivia Questions - Progress towards Trivia Pet and other Trivia Store resources. There are also vote related Achievements

*Clue Scrolls - Try to keep up with Clue scrolls, prioritizing 6x Beginner, and 1x Easy. Then do as many beginner clues as you can before doing any easy clues.

*Crystal Keys/Parts - During any PVMing, pick up any/all Crystal keys and parts.

*Building a Game plan - Review the Paragon League and read through each Relic Tier reward to get an idea of what direction you are going to pursue. Whether you're a skiller, PVMer, or somewhere in-between, developing a plan will help make your leagues experience as enjoyable as it can be.

*Spend your Paragon Points - Paragon Points can be assigned and unassigned at will. Don't forget to use them!

## T1 Rush (1,400 points)

## Disclaimer
The League Points total does not take into account any lucky drops from a Vote Boss, World Boss, chest opening, or slayer monster drop in the spirit of trying to be as conservative as possible. If you find yourself hitting the T1 or T2 threshold before the guide gets there, teleport home and select your Relic and off yo
[... 2370 more chars ...]

## Prestige
50px
The ability to Prestige your skills brings a new dynamic to the gameplay loop of leveling up skills. Prestige unlocks a wealth of new content, Talent points, and direct buffs to your player's stats.

To Prestige a skill, you must first reach level 99. Each subsequent prestige will require that you level up your skills 2 levels higher than the previous prestige requirement.
The table below will outline the level requirements for each available Prestige.

*Only available to specific Game modes.

## Prestige Benefits
Every skill will benefit from Prestige. You are recommended to always prestige every skill once you reach the level requirement.
*Leveling a skill beyond the prestige requirement is not recommended as there is no additional talent point gain for over-leveling.

## Universal Benefits
The following benefits are universal to all skill prestiges:
* Gain bonus Talent points when you prestige.
** The amount of talent points you receive when you prestige a skill is dependent on the Game mode you chose when creating your account.
** PvMer accounts will gain 20% more Prestige bonus talent progress in Combat skills.
** Skiller accounts will gain 20% more Prestige bonus talent progress in Skilling skills.
** Masochist accounts will gain 10% more Prestige bonus talent progress in All skills.
** Slayer Locked accounts will gain 20% more Prestige bonus talent progress in Combat skills.

## Combat Prestiges
Each time you Prestige a combat skill, you add an invisible Prestige bonus equal to the current and prior Prestige level requirement to your skill level.
* Your skill level is the level displayed in-game. This can be between 1 and 126.
* Your virtual skill level is calculated by adding your skill level together with your Prestige bonus.

If you prestige Magic for the first time, and then train to level 60 again, your virtual Magic level would be 159.
* This calculation works the same for higher prestige levels, with every prior prestige worth the levels being add
[... 896 more chars ...]

## Talent guides/PvM
August's talent system allows you to tailor your player power to the content you're working on, with your character growing stronger as you gain more talent points.

The guides provided in this section do not account for "situational utility" type talents such as Antifire or Antipoison. If you're fighting a boss that requires these, take them as necessary.

## Quick Guides 

## Melee 
* Melee early game
* Melee mid  game
* Melee late game (defensive)
* Melee late game (offensive)

## Ranged 
* Ranged early game
* Ranged mid game
* Ranged late game (defensive)
* Ranged late game (offensive)

## Magic 
* Magic early game
* Magic mid game
* Magic late game (defensive)
* Magic late game (offensive)

## Your First 50 Talent Points 

The first 50 talent points spent are going to be very similar for most builds, focusing on setting up a solid foundation for the early game.

## Tier 1 

* Depending on the attack style you want to focus on, pick one of Double Cast, Fluid Strikes, or Quick Shot.
* Put your 4 next points into Deft Strikes for bonus accuracy, and to unlock Tier 2 talents.

## Tier 2 

* Put at least one point into the DPS increase/proc talents for your spec:
** Melee:
*** Feral Fighter
*** Note: It is not recommended to put points into Giant Slayer early on, as you will not encounter many large, low level enemies.
** Ranged:
*** Ruthless Ranger
*** Headshot
** Magic:
*** Elementalist
*** Menacing Mage
* Focus on Consistency next, 5 levels of this talent will mean your minimum hit is 5, a huge DPS and xp gain boost.
* Fill out the Combat XP talent, as more levels gained means more talent points to spend.

From here, it's recommended to focus on filling out your DPS talents in Tier 2 until you unlock Tier 3 talents.

## Tier 3 

Your first picks into Tier 3 depend on a few factors:

* If you're playing an ironman, or want to start collecting extra gems and keys early, focus on Treasure Goblins.
* If you want to focus on slayer and get to pick your slayer tasks, f
[... 656 more chars ...]

## Talent guides/PvM/Melee early game
This build uses a total of 42 PvM talent points, and aims to provide reasonably efficient early-game talent choices.

If you would rather have the ability to choose slayer tasks over receiving Treasure Goblins, pick Unnatural Selection over Treasure Goblins.

You can alternatively max out the Feral Fighter talent rather than Consistency if you want a higher maximum potential DPS with, rather than more consistent DPS.

## Talent build 

Category: Talent builds

## Talent guides/PvM/Melee late game
This PvM build uses 303 talent points to maximize DPS for no-damage encounters and Slayer.

## Talent build 

Category: Talent builds

## Talent guides/PvM/Melee late game defensive
This PvM build uses 323 talent points to maximize defensive talents, while still providing solid DPS.

You can swap the Tier 5 talents Guardian and Executioner as needed; Guardian provides consistent DPS, while Executioner offers a large burst to execute high-health enemies. 

For increased damage reduction, replace your Tier 5 pick with Absolute Unit.

Tekton's Rage and Ghommal's Might should be changed based on whether you're using one handed or two handed weapons.

## Talent build 

Category: Talent builds

## Talent guides/PvM/Melee late game offensive
This build uses 248 talent points to maximize DPS for no-damage encounters and Slayer.

Tekton's Rage and Ghommal's Might should be changed based on whether you're using one handed or two handed weapons.

## Talent build 

Category: Talent builds

## Talent guides/PvM/Melee mid game
This build uses 130 talent points to maximize DPS for no-damage encounters and Slayer, ideal for bosses like the Giant mole and Thermonuclear smoke devil.

Renewal and Holey Vestments provide near-infinite prayer regeneration, though some gear with a prayer bonus is needed to fully sustain an overhead prayer.

This build relies on your HP being low (using a Dwarven rock cake) to trigger Berserker’s DPS boost while Aggression and Precision help to exploit enemy weaknesses for extra damage.

When your enemy has no weaknesses, Unnatural Selection and Firebender are good alternatives to meet the Tier 5 point requirement.

Alternatively, if you're concerned about taking damage or don't want to use a Dwarven rock cake, you can replace your Tier 5 pick with Guardian or Executioner.

## Talent spec slot orb
left|50px The Talent spec slot orb is a utility item that, when used, grants the player an additional talent spec slot. Talent specs are separate loadouts that can be swapped between freely at either the Talent Manager NPC at Home, or with the Talent spec trinket outside of instances and outside of combat.

## Talents
50px Drawing inspiration from leagues in Old School Runescape, August has taken the concept of relics and built upon it with our own unique twists to build out what we call our talent system. 

## Talents

## What are Talents? 
Talents are powerful buffs that allow you to enhance your character, including upgrades to your combat prowess, XP gain, and much more. A number of these talents work together to enhance your player's abilities.

Talents are grouped into three categories: PvM talents, Skilling talents, and Utility talents. In order to unlock these talents, you'll need to earn talent points.

## Talent Points 
Talent points are used to level up and unlock Talents. They can be gained by leveling up your skills, Prestiging your skills, or consuming Talent point orbs.

By default, talent points are earned every 40 levels gained. Based on the Game mode you chose when starting your character, you will gain certain talent points at a faster rate.
A quick reference chart is below for talent point bonuses per game mode. A 10% bonus means that talent points are earned every 36 levels instead of every 40.

## Talent Tier Requirements

## PvM Talent Tree
The PvM talent tree has a total of 6 Tiers which you can unlock. Unlocking the next tier requires that you have used a preset amount of talent points in previous tiers. 

## Skilling Talent Tree
The Skilling talent tree has a total of 5 Tiers which you can unlock. Unlocking the next tier requires that you have used a preset amount of talent points in previous tiers. 

## Utility Talent Tree
The Utility talent tree has a total of 6 Tiers which you can unlock. Unlocking the next tier requires that you have used a preset amount of talent points in previous tiers.

## Talents/Skilling talent tree
## Tier 1 
Each talent level in this tier costs 1 point.

## Tier 2 
At least 5 talent points must be spent in the previous tier to unlock tier 2 talents. Each talent level in this tier costs 2 points.

## Tier 3 
At least 25 talent points must be spent in the previous tiers to unlock tier 3 talents. Each talent level in this tier costs 3 points.

## Tier 4 
At least 55 talent points must be spent in the previous tiers to unlock tier 4 talents. Each talent level in this tier costs 4 points.

## Tier 5 
At least 105 talent points must be spent in the previous tiers to unlock tier 5 talents. Each talent level in this tier costs 6 points.

## Talents/Utility talent tree
## Tier 1 
Each talent level in this tier costs 2 points.

## Tier 2 
At least 20 talent points must be spent in the previous tier to unlock tier 2 talents. Each talent level in this tier costs 3 points.

## Tier 3 
At least 50 talent points must be spent in the previous tiers to unlock tier 3 talents. Each talent level in this tier costs 4 points.

## Tier 4 
At least 90 talent points must be spent in the previous tiers to unlock tier 4 talents. Each talent level in this tier costs 5 points.

## Tier 5 
At least 115 talent points must be spent in the previous tiers to unlock tier 5 talents. Each talent level in this tier costs 6 points.

## Tier 6 
At least 175 talent points must be spent in the previous tiers to unlock tier 6 talents. Each talent level in this tier costs 10 points.

## Voting
64px Voting is a free and easy method of supporting the growth of August RSPS, and also gain some rewards! Votes can be cast for August RSPS once every 12 hours, per website. You receive a reward for each site you vote on, with the maximum reward for voting on all 3 vote websites; RuneLocus, Rune Server, and RuneList.

This means you can vote a total of 3 times ever 12 hours, or 6 times per 24 hours. 

Voting can be done by clicking the vote button on the August RSPS website or by typing ::vote in-game to be automatically redirected.

To claim your votes, type ::claimvotes in game.

## Vote Rewards
In order to receive your rewards and maintain you Vote Streak, players must claim their votes in game. 
*To claim your votes, type ::claimvotes in game.

## Vote Buff
Every time you vote, you will receive the following buffs.
{| class="wikitable sortable"
! Buff Description
|-
| +5% talent point progress
|-
| +100% AFK zone xp
|-
| +20% xp when in close proximity to a group member
|-
| +1% drop rate per site voted on
|-
|Access to Shooting stars
|}

## Daily Vote Rewards
Once per day, if you've voted for August, you will unlock a new vote reward for the current month!
*Visit the Vote Obelisk next to the Rejuvenation Pool to see and claim your rewards.
**This shop resets at the end of each calendar month!

## Vote Streak
If you vote consecutively without missing a day, you'll unlock a Vote Streak at certain thresholds.Vote streaks will award Vote Streak Points that can be spent at the Vote streak store for exclusive rewards.
*Remember! You must claim your votes in order for the streak to count! Simply voting on the sites does not add to your Vote Streak.

A Vote streak is started once a player has voted for 7 consecutive days. 
After 1 week, you will be awarded 1 vote streak point. You will receive 2 vote streak points for a 2 week long streak, and 3 vote streak points for any streaks past 3+ weeks.
